zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Rudhall於1486年出生在英國賀福爾洛--也時，她的父親William Rudhall26歲，母親Anne Milbourne22歲。她於大約1515年與Richard Reade在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1625年在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾享年139歲，埋葬在英國布里斯托。

nameMeanings.name: Rudhall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Rudhall was born in 1486, in Ross-on-Wye, Herefordshire, England, her father, William Rudhall, was 26 and her mother, Anne Milbourne, was 22. She married Richard Reade about 1515, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1625, in Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 139, and was buried in Bristol, England.

Elle a épousé Richard Reade environ 1515, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1625, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 139 ans, et a été enterrée à Bristol, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Rudhallが1486年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアロス-オン-ウイで生まれた時，父William Rudhallは26歳，母Anne Milbourneは22歳であった。約1515年にイギリスイギリスでRichard Readeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1625年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアで139歳で死去し，イギリスブリストルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Rudhall(이)가 1486년 영국 허퍼드실르 로스--w예에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Rudhall(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Milbourne(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1515년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Richard Reade(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1625년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르에서 139세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 브리스톨에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Rudhall родилась в 1486 г. в местности росс-он-ви, хирефордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Rudhall было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Milbourne, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Reade около 1515 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1625 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 139 лет и была похоронена в местности бристол, Англия.
